title: tarenna gracilipes var. kotoensis
intro: Tarenna gracilipes var. kotoensis, a variant of the Tarenna gracilipes plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Tarenna gracilipes var. kotoensis include its potential to aid in the treatment of malaria, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
